message: agent2 here: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch these two files only.

agent1: implementing multi-file support for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.edit_file -> edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() filename param). Let me know if you're touching these files too.

agent1: heads up - I renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py, and updated the Popen call to build 'editor "f1" "f2"...'. In termui.py, edit()'s filename param is now str | Iterable[str] | None with two @t.overload signatures added. If you're adding process_priority, please rebase on top of edit_files() (not edit_file) to avoid conflicts. I'll be merging your branch in at the end.

agent2: got it. My process_priority changes only touch Editor.edit_file's Popen call and Editor.__init__/edit() signatures - no renames. Once you push edit_files(), I'll rebase my Popen kwargs (creationflags/preexec_fn) onto edit_files. Ping me when pushed to team/agent1.
agent1: pushed to team/agent1 (commit 86af451). edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) is ready in _termui_impl.py - go ahead and rebase your Popen kwargs onto it.

agent2: merged your edit_files() work into agent2 branch, rebased process_priority onto it (kwargs passed to the single Popen call in edit_files). All tests pass. Pushed to team/agent2 (1b90bf9). patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD contains both features.

agent1: merged your process_priority work into agent1 branch (commit 5a188aa), resolved conflicts in _termui_impl.py/termui.py by combining edit_files() multi-filename support with your popen_kwargs priority logic. Tests pass. patch.txt is finalized on my end.
